atlas.reason: PROPOSED_ATLAS entries are not product-supported or AI-authorable.

limitations: Simple observed speed derivative from two displacement-derived velocity windows only.
Second derivatives amplify tracking noise; missing endpoints or implausible spikes produce UNKNOWN.
Does not infer effort, sprint quality, physical capacity, intent, role, pressure-breaking quality, or tactical causation.
Acceleration/deceleration PASS means the observed speed change exceeded frozen thresholds, not that a player deliberately burst or braked.
runtime_contract.name: acceleration

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where acceleration is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Anchor-record field containing the tracked player id.
parameters.name: entity_id_field

parameters.description: Length of each non-overlapping displacement-velocity window.
parameters.name: lookback_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum absolute speed change required for acceleration/deceleration PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_abs_delta_speed_mps

parameters.description: Minimum absolute acceleration magnitude required for PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_abs_acceleration_mps2

parameters.description: Frozen plausibility limit for velocity-window speeds.
parameters.name: maximum_player_speed_mps

parameters.description: Frozen plausibility limit used to mark likely tracking-noise spikes UNKNOWN.
parameters.name: maximum_abs_acceleration_mps2

runtime_contract.purpose: Estimate observed entity speed-up and slow-down around an anchor-relative frame from two non-overlapping displacement-velocity windows.

limitations: Simple observed speed derivative from two displacement-derived velocity windows only.
Second derivatives amplify tracking noise; missing endpoints or implausible spikes produce UNKNOWN.
Does not infer effort, sprint quality, physical capacity, intent, role, pressure-breaking quality, or tactical causation.
Acceleration/deceleration PASS means the observed speed change exceeded frozen thresholds, not that a player deliberately burst or braked.

limitations: Claims only observed ball-target lane screening geometry at the declared frame.
PASS means at least one observed candidate defender lies between ball and target within the frozen lane-distance buffer.
Does not infer defender intent, tactical denial quality, pass probability, pitch-control value, marking assignment, scheme, causation, or optimality.
Missing ball/target tracking, too-short lanes, invalid thresholds, or insufficient observed defender tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than a lane-denial claim.
runtime_contract.name: cover_shadow

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where ball-target lane screening is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Anchor field containing the tracked target entity that defines the lane from the ball.
parameters.name: target_entity_field

parameters.description: Observed outfield candidate group checked for defender-lane screening geometry.
parameters.name: candidate_scope

parameters.description: Maximum perpendicular distance from ball-target lane for cover_shadow_status PASS.
parameters.name: maximum_lane_distance_m

parameters.description: Minimum normalized along-lane projection distance away from the ball and target endpoints.
parameters.name: minimum_projection_fraction

parameters.description: Minimum ball-target lane length before screening geometry is evaluated.
parameters.name: minimum_lane_length_m

parameters.description: Minimum observed candidate defenders before PASS/FAIL lane-screening status is claimed.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_defenders

runtime_contract.purpose: Evaluate observed defender screening geometry on the ball-to-target lane at an anchor-relative frame.

limitations: Claims only that two evidence rows joined under declared key/status/time/distinctness/same-entity constraints.
Does not infer tactical intent, causation, role semantics, optimality, or decision quality.
Preserves upstream UNKNOWN/FAIL status instead of treating missing evidence as support for a tactical claim.
N-way tactical compositions must be expressed as chains of this binary join.
runtime_contract.name: join_episode_sets

parameters.description: Field read from each left episode to produce the join key.
parameters.name: left_key_field

parameters.description: Field read from each right episode to produce the join key.
parameters.name: right_key_field

parameters.description: Optional status field on the left episode that must equal required_status_value.
parameters.name: left_status_field

parameters.description: Optional status field on the right episode that must equal required_status_value.
parameters.name: right_status_field

parameters.description: Status value required when a left or right status field is configured.
parameters.name: required_status_value

parameters.description: Optional temporal relation between joined episodes.
parameters.name: temporal_relation

parameters.description: Left frame field used by temporal relations that compare point frames.
parameters.name: left_time_field

parameters.description: Right frame field used by temporal relations that compare point frames.
parameters.name: right_time_field

parameters.description: Maximum allowed right_time - left_time gap when temporal_relation checks order.
parameters.name: maximum_gap_seconds

parameters.description: Comma-separated entity-reference fields that must be pairwise distinct after projection.
parameters.name: distinct_entity_fields

parameters.description: Entity-reference field pair that must be equal after projection.
parameters.name: same_entity_fields

runtime_contract.purpose: Binary reusable composition primitive that joins two episode sets by declared evidence-key fields, with optional status, temporal-order, and pairwise entity-distinctness constraints.

limitations: Claims only observed nearest-opposition proximity to a target player at the declared frame.
marking_status PASS means the nearest observed candidate is within the frozen distance threshold; unmarked_status PASS means the nearest observed candidate is outside it.
Does not infer marking assignment, defensive scheme, man-or-zone responsibility, role, intent, communication, causation, quality, or optimality.
Missing target tracking or insufficient observed candidate tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than a marked/unmarked claim.
runtime_contract.name: marking

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where observed marking proximity is evaluated.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Anchor field containing the target player being checked for observed nearest-opponent proximity.
parameters.name: target_player_id_field

parameters.description: Observed outfield candidate group from which the nearest marker is selected.
parameters.name: candidate_scope

parameters.description: Nearest-opponent distance threshold for marking_status PASS.
parameters.name: maximum_marking_distance_m

parameters.description: Minimum observed outfield opponents before marked/unmarked can be claimed.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_marker_candidates

runtime_contract.purpose: Evaluate observed nearest-opposition proximity to an anchor target player at an anchor-relative frame.

limitations: Claims only observed endpoint-window off-ball movement by a non-ball actor in the declared candidate scope.
PASS requires clear endpoint tracking, displacement, average speed, and distance from the ball under frozen thresholds.
Does not infer run type, run purpose, decoy movement, marker dragging, space creation, role, intent, tactical causation, quality, or optimality.
Directional/path labels are separate derived claims; only explicitly modeled observed path geometry may compose on top of this base movement episode.
Insufficient candidate tracking or missing ball endpoints produce UNKNOWN rather than a fabricated run/no-run claim.
runtime_contract.name: off_ball_run

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field where the run observation window starts.
parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Observed outfield candidate group evaluated for off-ball movement.
parameters.name: candidate_scope

parameters.description: Forward tracking window used to measure endpoint displacement.
parameters.name: lookahead_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum endpoint displacement required for off_ball_run_status PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_run_displacement_m

parameters.description: Minimum endpoint-average speed required for off_ball_run_status PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_run_speed_mps

parameters.description: Minimum distance from the ball at both endpoints for the candidate to count as off-ball.
parameters.name: minimum_ball_distance_m

parameters.description: Minimum candidate players observed at both endpoints before PASS/FAIL can be claimed.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_candidates

parameters.description: Maximum endpoint-missing candidate ratio before the run observation is UNKNOWN.
parameters.name: maximum_missing_candidate_ratio

runtime_contract.purpose: Detect an observed off-ball movement episode for the best same-team outfield candidate over a short anchor-relative tracking window.

limitations: Classifies only observed path geometry over an already observed off-ball run episode.
run_in_behind_status means the runner moved from not beyond to beyond the observed opposition outfield line under frozen thresholds.
diagonal_run_status means the run had both forward and lateral components under frozen thresholds.
Does not infer decoy movement, marker dragging, space creation, overlap/underlap role, third-player tactical purpose, intent, causation, quality, or optimality.
Insufficient line or tracking coverage produces UNKNOWN for line-relative labels.
runtime_contract.name: off_ball_run_type

parameters.description: Minimum forward component for supported forward path labels.
parameters.name: minimum_forward_progression_m

parameters.description: Minimum lateral component for diagonal_run_status PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_lateral_displacement_m

parameters.description: Minimum observed opposition outfield defenders at each endpoint before run-in-behind can be evaluated.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_defenders

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify observed off-ball run path geometry for supported literal run labels such as run-in-behind and diagonal run.

limitations: Classifies only observed path geometry over an already observed off-ball run episode.
run_in_behind_status means the runner moved from not beyond to beyond the observed opposition outfield line under frozen thresholds.
diagonal_run_status means the run had both forward and lateral components under frozen thresholds.
Does not infer decoy movement, marker dragging, space creation, overlap/underlap role, third-player tactical purpose, intent, causation, quality, or optimality.
Insufficient line or tracking coverage produces UNKNOWN for line-relative labels.
runtime_contract.name: off_ball_run_type

parameters.name: minimum_forward_progression_m

parameters.description: Minimum lateral component for diagonal_run_status PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_lateral_displacement_m

parameters.description: Minimum observed opposition outfield defenders at each endpoint before run-in-behind can be evaluated.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_defenders

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify observed off-ball run path geometry for supported literal run labels such as run-in-behind and diagonal run.

limitations: Does not infer intent, optimality, or missed opportunity.
runtime_contract.name: outcome_classification

parameters.description: Outcome lookahead horizon in seconds.
parameters.name: horizon

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify the post-anchor outcome under the frozen M1 predicates.

limitations: Trusted recipe wrapper; agent-authored plans should use relation_destination_entry.
Requires an upstream relation episode set.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
runtime_contract.name: relation_destination_entry_classification

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for destination lane entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic experimental result IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic selection strategy when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

runtime_contract.purpose: Classify whether the ball later enters the destination region of a previously evaluated relation episode.

limitations: Provider event taxonomy supplies restart/set-piece identity; tracking supplies arrangement evidence.
PASS means a recognized restart event has enough observed outfield players for both teams at the anchor frame.
Does not infer routine variant, planned play, delivery pattern, marking assignment, player role, intent, quality, or tactical causation.
runtime_contract.name: set_piece_structure

parameters.description: Minimum observed outfield players per team required to claim arrangement evidence.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_outfield_players

runtime_contract.purpose: Expose recognized provider restart/set-piece events with observed attacking and defending outfield arrangement at the aligned event frame.

limitations: Provider event taxonomy supplies restart/set-piece identity; tracking supplies arrangement evidence.
PASS means a recognized restart event has enough observed outfield players for both teams at the anchor frame.
Does not infer routine variant, planned play, delivery pattern, marking assignment, player role, intent, quality, or tactical causation.
runtime_contract.name: set_piece_structure

parameters.description: Minimum observed outfield players per team required to claim arrangement evidence.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_outfield_players

runtime_contract.purpose: Expose recognized provider restart/set-piece events with observed attacking and defending outfield arrangement at the aligned event frame.

limitations: Claims only sampled open-space candidate geometry from observed outfield-player positions at a declared frame.
PASS means at least the frozen number of sampled pitch points satisfy frozen nearest-player distance thresholds.
Does not infer continuous polygons, pitch control, space value, creation, exploitation, player intent, tactical causation, quality, or optimality.
Invalid direction, invalid grid parameters, or insufficient observed player tracking produces UNKNOWN rather than an open/closed-space claim.
runtime_contract.name: space_region_generation

parameters.name: frame_field

parameters.description: Optional attacking-direction-normalized region in which candidate points are sampled.
parameters.name: zone_scope

parameters.description: Pitch-grid spacing for sampled candidate open-space points.
parameters.name: grid_step_m

parameters.description: Minimum distance from every observed opponent outfielder for a sampled point to count as open.
parameters.name: minimum_opponent_distance_m

parameters.description: Minimum distance from every observed teammate outfielder for a sampled point to count as open.
parameters.name: minimum_teammate_distance_m

parameters.description: Minimum sampled candidate points required for open_space_status PASS.
parameters.name: minimum_open_points

parameters.description: Maximum representative candidate points retained as evidence.
parameters.name: maximum_candidate_points

parameters.description: Minimum observed outfield players for each team before sampled open-space status is claimed.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_players_per_team

runtime_contract.purpose: Generate observed open-space candidate geometry from sampled pitch points at an anchor-relative frame.

limitations: Claims only observed multi-defender pressure geometry around a carrier at the declared frame.
PASS means multiple observed defenders satisfy frozen distance, closing/approach, and angular-spread thresholds.
Does not infer press trap, pressing trigger, coordinated scheme, defensive communication, intent, causation, pressure quality, or optimality.
Missing carrier tracking, insufficient defender tracking, or missing kinematic evidence produces UNKNOWN rather than a team-press claim.
runtime_contract.name: team_press

parameters.name: minimum_closing_speed_mps

parameters.description: Maximum angle between defender velocity and defender-to-carrier vector.
parameters.name: maximum_approach_angle_degrees

parameters.description: Minimum observed defenders satisfying pressure actor criteria.
parameters.name: minimum_pressing_defenders

parameters.description: Minimum angular separation among pressure actors around the carrier.
parameters.name: minimum_angle_spread_degrees

parameters.description: Minimum observed defending outfield players before PASS/FAIL team-press status is claimed.
parameters.name: minimum_observed_defenders

parameters.description: Velocity lookback interval for closing-speed and approach-angle estimates.
parameters.name: lookback_seconds

parameters.name: candidate_scope
